source_intake_record.raw_question_text:
Ella wants to write a number into each cell of a $3 \times 3$ grid so that the sum of the numbers in any two cells that share an edge is the same. She has already written two numbers, as shown in the diagram.
When Ella has completed the grid, what will be the sum of all the
<image1>
numbers in the grid?

image_dependency: question_text explicitly says 'as shown in the diagram'
The locations and values of the two pre-filled numbers are not given in text
asset_registry_record.image_manifest shows 1 existing image and registry_passed=true
multi_step: Solving requires using a global adjacency constraint ('sum of any two cells that share an edge is the same') across a 3x3 grid
The problem also requires incorporating the two pre-filled diagram values/positions, so it is not a single direct lookup
Final target is the sum of all 9 cells, implying derivation from intermediate cell relationships rather than immediate reading
Conservative scoring because the exact diagram values and placements are not available in the text
verifiability: answer_type is numeric and answer_text='22', so the expected output format is precise
The constraint-based setup suggests a determinate mathematical result that can be checked by reconstruction or algebra

risk_flags: cannot directly inspect image content from provided fields, so reasoning complexity is estimated conservatively
diagram-specific givens are omitted from question text

decision_reasons: Core assets are present: question text, image, and numeric answer all exist.
